data.body:
Optionality validation completed offline (commit 045b2d77). The hard rule — LLM stack optional for ALL interfaces (library/CLI/MCP) x ALL operations (review_ticket/review_code/scan_epics_for_spec) — is now proven by a dedicated exhaustive guard: tests/interfaces/store/test_llm_optionality.py (15 tests, offline). It asserts (a) import-cleanliness for every interface entrypoint (import rebar / rebar._cli / rebar.mcp_server / rebar.llm pull NO agents stack), (b) graceful degradation per interface x operation with asserted messages (typed LLMError | CLI Error:+exit1 | gated MCP tool error), (c) an exhaustiveness check that DISCOVERS runner-backed ops from the public surface so a new op cannot ship without coverage.

Subagent review found + I fixed a real silent-success bug: scan_epics_for_spec over a zero-epic store never invoked the runner, so a missing extra returned an empty-but-successful result. Fix: cheap offline Runner.preflight() (import-only readiness check) called up front in scan_epics_for_spec and review_code; a zero-work workload now fails loudly on a missing extra. Regression test added.

The LIVE-model acceptance items in this ticket (structured_response population, no-temperature-400 on claude-opus-4.x, MCP session lifecycle, Langfuse trace+flush) still require real API keys and remain executable only via .github/workflows/external-integration.yml (pytest -m external tests/external; key secret required, fails loudly if unset) — this sandbox has no key. Those are deferred to that dispatchable CI job. The OPTIONALITY half of this ticket's intent (the user's hard requirement) is fully validated offline here. Full non-integration suite green: 1829 passed, 7 skipped.
